name: Khalil Center
mission: Our wellness centers integrate Islamic spiritual approaches into professional psychology to help achieve optimal, holistic health by providing a wide variety of mental health services.
theoryOfChange: Khalil Center utilizes the Traditional Islamically Integrated Psychotherapy (TIIP) framework, which is a therapeutic model developed by their leadership. This framework integrates professional psychology into an Islamic context, outlining Islamic beliefs regarding health, pathology, human ontology, and epistemology. It provides a refined integrative approach within an Islamic framework for psychotherapeutic practice and guides their services and research.
scoreSummary: Khalil Foundation shows good alignment and good impact, with zakat compliance (minor risk deductions applied).

evaluationSources: Practice-based evidence study published in 'Spirituality in Clinical Practice' (2023)
Research published in the Journal of Muslim Mental Health

dimension_explanations.alignment: With a score of 31/50, the foundation shows medium to high alignment for Muslim donors due to its specialized focus on global health and religious sensitivity [2]. It maintains a clear zakat policy and provides essential community education on mental health topics [2][6].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization demonstrates moderate impact with a score of 28/50, focusing on capacity building through psychotherapy sessions and professional training in TIIP [4][5]. While program delivery is consistent, the cost per beneficiary is approximately $907.87, and the organization maintains a program expense ratio of 71.2% [1][3].

strengths: High fundraising efficiency with only $0.01 spent to raise every $1

Unique integration of Traditional Islamically Integrated Psychotherapy (TIIP)
baseline_narrative.summary: The Khalil Foundation, operating as the Khalil Center, provides specialized clinical mental health services and community education rooted in Islamic psychology [1][4]. The organization is recognized as zakat-eligible, utilizing funds to support mental health care for those in need according to established asnaf categories [2].

strengths.detail: The organization demonstrates remarkable fiscal discipline, spending only <cite id="6">$0.01 to raise every $1 in donations</cite>, which is significantly better than industry averages.
A substantial <cite id="7">71.2% of total expenses</cite> are directed toward clinical services and community education rather than administrative overhead.
The center has delivered <cite id="8">70,136 lifetime psychotherapy sessions</cite> and hosted <cite id="9">52 support groups</cite> to address community-wide trauma and wellness.

strengths_deep_dive: The Khalil Center's primary strength lies in its dual-track approach of direct service and professional capacity building. While they provide intensive clinical care, they also focus on systemic change by training the next generation of practitioners. To date, they have <cite id="10">trained 500 professionals in the TIIP framework</cite>, ensuring that their methodology can be replicated beyond their own clinics. This is supported by a robust governance structure where <cite id="11">100% of the board members are independent</cite>, ensuring objective oversight of their mission. Furthermore, their financial model is diversified; while they received <cite id="12">$796,691 in contributions</cite>, they also generated <cite id="13">$1,476,207 through program service revenue</cite>, indicating a sustainable demand for their specialized clinical evaluations and therapy sessions.
rich_narrative.summary: The Khalil Foundation, operating as the Khalil Center, is a specialized mental health organization that bridges the gap between modern clinical psychology and traditional Islamic spirituality. Founded in <cite id="1">2010</cite>, the center addresses the unique psychological needs of Muslim communities through its proprietary <cite id="2">Traditional Islamically Integrated Psychotherapy (TIIP) framework</cite>. This model provides a culturally sensitive alternative to secular mental health care, which often fails to account for the spiritual dimensions of a Muslim's life. The organization operates at a significant scale for a specialized provider, reporting <cite id="3">$2,272,898 in total revenue</cite> for the 2023 tax year. Their reach is international, with physical and digital services spanning the <cite id="4">United States, Canada, the United Kingdom, Pakistan, Turkey, and Qatar</cite>. By integrating faith with clinical practice, the Khalil Center serves as both a direct service provider and a research institution, having established the <cite id="5">International Association of Islamic Psychology</cite> to standardize this emerging field. Their work is particularly vital given the high stigma surrounding mental health in many immigrant and minority communities, providing a safe, faith-aligned space for healing.
